independent_variables.name: Distance from road
independent_variables.notes: Good for transects away from roads. Usually systematic sampling.

independent_variables.name: Distance from park entrance
independent_variables.notes: Good for litter, human disturbance, and vegetation patterns in parks.

independent_variables.name: Distance from water edge
independent_variables.notes: Good for soil moisture, vegetation cover, species richness, and microclimate.

independent_variables.name: Distance from footpath
independent_variables.notes: Good for studying trampling and human disturbance.

independent_variables.name: Distance from trash bin
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing whether bins reduce litter density nearby.

independent_variables.name: Distance downstream from discharge point
independent_variables.notes: Only use if the sampling site is safe and legal.

independent_variables.name: Surface type
independent_variables.notes: Good for urban heat and runoff/infiltration studies.

independent_variables.name: Land-use type
independent_variables.notes: Broad but flexible. Needs clear site selection.

independent_variables.name: Habitat type
independent_variables.notes: Good for biodiversity and species richness.

independent_variables.name: Management type
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing management strategies.

independent_variables.name: Shading type
independent_variables.notes: Good for microclimate and urban heat studies.

independent_variables.name: Traffic intensity zone
independent_variables.notes: Good when exact road distance is hard to measure.

independent_variables.name: Time of day
independent_variables.notes: Good for time-based changes, but weather and daily variation need control.

independent_variables.name: Weekday vs weekend
independent_variables.notes: Needs enough repeats because only two categories is weak by itself.

independent_variables.name: Before vs after rainfall
independent_variables.notes: Good but weather-dependent and harder to schedule.

independent_variables.name: Nitrate concentration
independent_variables.notes: Good for duckweed/algae growth experiments.

independent_variables.name: Phosphate concentration
independent_variables.notes: Good for eutrophication-style lab investigations.

independent_variables.name: Salt concentration
independent_variables.notes: Good for plant germination or growth under salinity stress.

independent_variables.name: pH level
independent_variables.notes: Good for acidification simulations.

independent_variables.name: Fertilizer concentration
independent_variables.notes: Good for plant growth, but runoff/eutrophication link should be clear.

independent_variables.name: Compost percentage
independent_variables.notes: Good for soil quality and plant growth.

independent_variables.name: Biochar percentage
independent_variables.notes: Good for soil water retention or plant growth.

independent_variables.name: Light intensity
independent_variables.notes: Can be lab or fieldwork depending on design.

independent_variables.name: Temperature
independent_variables.notes: May be hard to control without incubators.

independent_variables.name: Mulch thickness
independent_variables.notes: Good for soil moisture and temperature.

independent_variables.name: Age group
independent_variables.notes: Use carefully and avoid overgeneralizing from small samples.

independent_variables.name: Grade level
independent_variables.notes: Useful in school-based surveys.

independent_variables.name: Frequency of recycling
independent_variables.notes: Can be IV or DV depending on survey design.

independent_variables.name: Transport mode
independent_variables.notes: Useful for behavior and carbon footprint-style surveys.

independent_variables.name: GDP per capita
independent_variables.notes: Useful for cross-country secondary data investigations.

independent_variables.name: Urbanization rate
independent_variables.notes: Good for secondary data, but needs reliable sources.

independent_variables.name: Forest cover percentage
independent_variables.notes: Good for deforestation/conservation topics.

independent_variables.name: Protected area coverage
independent_variables.notes: Good for conservation-focused secondary data.

independent_variables.name: Year
independent_variables.notes: Good for time-series secondary data.

independent_variables.name: Distance from major intersection
independent_variables.notes: Good for traffic-related air pollution and noise studies near junctions.

independent_variables.name: Road type
independent_variables.notes: Useful when road distance is difficult to standardize.

independent_variables.name: Presence of roadside vegetation
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing whether vegetation changes air quality, temperature, or noise.

independent_variables.name: Indoor vs outdoor location
independent_variables.notes: Works best with CO₂, PM2.5, temperature, or humidity.

independent_variables.name: Ventilation condition
independent_variables.notes: Good for classroom CO₂ or indoor PM investigations. Keep occupancy similar.

independent_variables.name: Distance from storm drain
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing runoff effects after rainfall, if sampling is safe and legal.

independent_variables.name: Surrounding land use near water body
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing water quality between sites with different land uses.

independent_variables.name: Water body type
independent_variables.notes: Useful, but students should avoid comparing sites that differ in too many uncontrolled ways.

independent_variables.name: Shading over water
independent_variables.notes: Good for water temperature, DO, algae, or light penetration.

independent_variables.name: Flow rate category
independent_variables.notes: Works better if students can measure or clearly define flow categories.

independent_variables.name: Park management intensity
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing vegetation, species richness, and habitat quality.

independent_variables.name: Mowing frequency
independent_variables.notes: Good for grassland/park biodiversity or pollinator studies.

independent_variables.name: Flower abundance category
independent_variables.notes: Good for pollinator visit frequency or invertebrate abundance.

independent_variables.name: Native vs ornamental planting
independent_variables.notes: Good if students can identify or classify planting types reliably.

independent_variables.name: Distance from human disturbance source
independent_variables.notes: Use for benches, playgrounds, entrances, paths, or gathering areas.

independent_variables.name: Soil depth
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing soil moisture, pH, nutrients, or organic matter by depth.

independent_variables.name: Distance from tree trunk
independent_variables.notes: Good for soil moisture, pH, temperature, leaf litter, or vegetation cover.

independent_variables.name: Soil type
independent_variables.notes: Good for water-holding capacity, infiltration, or plant growth.

independent_variables.name: Irrigation frequency
independent_variables.notes: Good for plant growth or seedling survival experiments.

independent_variables.name: Organic fertilizer amount
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing plant growth while linking to sustainable agriculture.

independent_variables.name: Soil compaction treatment
independent_variables.notes: Good for infiltration rate, germination, or root length.

independent_variables.name: Distance from tree canopy edge
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing the cooling or shading effect of trees.

independent_variables.name: Percentage canopy cover
independent_variables.notes: Good for temperature, humidity, light intensity, or soil moisture.

independent_variables.name: Building density
independent_variables.notes: Useful for urban heat island studies if categories are clearly defined.

independent_variables.name: Surface color
independent_variables.notes: Good for surface temperature and albedo-style investigations.

independent_variables.name: Distance from building wall
independent_variables.notes: Good for wall heat, shade, or wind shelter effects.

independent_variables.name: Green space size
independent_variables.notes: Good for city/park comparisons using GIS or online datasets.

independent_variables.name: Impervious surface percentage
independent_variables.notes: Useful for runoff, urban heat, or green space investigations.

independent_variables.name: Distance from food vendor or cafeteria
independent_variables.notes: Good for litter studies in school or public food areas.

independent_variables.name: Bin visibility
independent_variables.notes: Good for whether bin placement affects litter density.

independent_variables.name: Bin type
independent_variables.notes: Good for recycling contamination or waste sorting studies.

independent_variables.name: Presence of waste-sorting signage
independent_variables.notes: Good for sorting accuracy or recycling knowledge/behavior studies.

independent_variables.name: Time after lunch period
independent_variables.notes: Good for cafeteria or school litter studies.

independent_variables.name: Area type in school
independent_variables.notes: Useful for school-based human impact investigations.

independent_variables.name: Household size
independent_variables.notes: Useful for energy use, waste generation, or carbon footprint surveys.

independent_variables.name: Air conditioner use frequency
independent_variables.notes: Good for energy behavior surveys in hot climates.

independent_variables.name: Transport distance to school
independent_variables.notes: Good for school commute carbon footprint surveys.

independent_variables.name: Diet type
independent_variables.notes: Use sensitively and avoid judging individual choices.

independent_variables.name: Food origin
independent_variables.notes: Good for food miles or local food percentage studies.

independent_variables.name: Fertilizer type
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing plant growth under different fertilizer types.

independent_variables.name: Watering amount
independent_variables.notes: Good for seedling survival, plant height, or biomass.

independent_variables.name: Crop type
independent_variables.notes: Better for comparing responses to the same treatment than as a standalone IV.

independent_variables.name: Environmental club participation
independent_variables.notes: Needs a good sample size because it has only two groups.

independent_variables.name: Science course level
independent_variables.notes: Useful for environmental knowledge or attitudes in school.

independent_variables.name: Prior environmental education
independent_variables.notes: Should be defined using clear survey questions, not guessed by the student.

independent_variables.name: Access to recycling facilities at home
independent_variables.notes: Good for recycling behavior or waste sorting knowledge studies.

independent_variables.name: Frequency of outdoor activity
independent_variables.notes: Useful for green space attitudes or conservation concern surveys.

independent_variables.name: Commute distance to school
independent_variables.notes: Good for school transport, carbon footprint, or travel behavior surveys.

independent_variables.name: Commute time to school
independent_variables.notes: Useful when exact distance is hard for students to estimate.

independent_variables.name: Access to public transport
independent_variables.notes: Good for investigating whether infrastructure affects sustainable transport choices.

independent_variables.name: Use of food delivery services
independent_variables.notes: Good for single-use packaging, transport emissions, and food waste survey topics.

independent_variables.name: Use of reusable water bottle
independent_variables.notes: Simple and realistic for school-based surveys about single-use plastic.

independent_variables.name: Frequency of cafeteria use
independent_variables.notes: Useful for school food waste or sustainable food behavior studies.

independent_variables.name: Exposure to environmental signage
independent_variables.notes: Good for linking school signs or campaigns to knowledge or behavior.

independent_variables.name: Participation in environmental activities
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing whether active participation is linked to stronger environmental attitudes.

independent_variables.name: Perceived convenience of recycling
independent_variables.notes: Useful for investigating barriers to recycling behavior.

independent_variables.name: Frequency of online shopping
independent_variables.notes: Good for packaging waste or consumer behavior investigations.

independent_variables.name: Frequency of buying new clothing
independent_variables.notes: Useful for fast fashion and sustainable consumption topics.

independent_variables.name: Air purifier use frequency
independent_variables.notes: Useful for perceived air pollution risk or indoor air quality behavior surveys.

independent_variables.name: Use of single-use plastic bottles
independent_variables.notes: Clear and measurable for school-based plastic waste surveys.

independent_variables.name: Perceived access to green space
independent_variables.notes: Useful for urban green space, wellbeing, and environmental behavior studies.

independent_variables.name: Household water-saving habit frequency
independent_variables.notes: Good for water conservation behavior surveys.

independent_variables.name: Human Development Index
independent_variables.notes: Useful for comparing environmental indicators with level of development.

independent_variables.name: Population density
independent_variables.notes: Good for urban pressure, resource use, waste, or pollution investigations.

independent_variables.name: Access to electricity
independent_variables.notes: Useful for development-energy relationships.

independent_variables.name: Coal energy percentage
independent_variables.notes: Good for carbon emissions or air pollution secondary data investigations.

independent_variables.name: Fossil fuel energy percentage
independent_variables.notes: Useful for comparing energy structure with emissions or pollution.

independent_variables.name: Education index
independent_variables.notes: Could link education level to sustainability indicators, but students need reliable datasets.

independent_variables.name: Meat supply per capita
independent_variables.notes: Useful for diet, food systems, land use, and carbon footprint investigations.

independent_variables.name: Fertilizer use per hectare
independent_variables.notes: Good for linking agriculture intensity to water pollution or eutrophication indicators.

independent_variables.name: Pesticide use per hectare
independent_variables.notes: Useful for biodiversity or agricultural pollution investigations.

independent_variables.name: Urban green space per capita
independent_variables.notes: Good for city-level comparisons of urban sustainability.

independent_variables.name: Industrial value added percentage
independent_variables.notes: Useful for comparing industrialization with pollution indicators.

independent_variables.name: Environmental Performance Index score
independent_variables.notes: Good as an IV or DV, but students must understand what the index represents.

independent_variables.name: Vehicle ownership rate
independent_variables.notes: Good for country or city comparisons with air pollution or carbon emissions.

independent_variables.name: Renewable energy investment
independent_variables.notes: Good for renewable energy percentage or CO₂ emissions studies.

independent_variables.name: Agricultural land percentage
independent_variables.notes: Useful for food systems, land use, water stress, or emissions studies.

independent_variables.name: Distance from windbreak
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing whether trees, hedges, walls, or buildings reduce wind speed or change local microclimate.

independent_variables.name: Windbreak presence
independent_variables.notes: Useful when measuring wind speed, temperature, or PM concentration in sheltered and unsheltered areas.

independent_variables.name: Slope angle
independent_variables.notes: Good for soil moisture, infiltration, runoff evidence, vegetation cover, or plant abundance.

independent_variables.name: Rainfall amount before sampling
independent_variables.notes: Usually better as context or a secondary-data IV. Good for turbidity, stream flow, soil moisture, or runoff investigations.

independent_variables.name: Presence of green wall
independent_variables.notes: Good for PM2.5, PM10, temperature, or public support investigations.

independent_variables.name: Distance from green wall
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing whether green walls affect PM concentration, surface temperature, or air temperature nearby.

independent_variables.name: Presence of pedestrianized area
independent_variables.notes: Good for PM2.5, PM10, noise, traffic count, or public support for pedestrian town centers.

independent_variables.name: Permeability of ground material
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing infiltration rate, runoff, soil moisture nearby, or surface temperature.

independent_variables.name: Soil amendment type
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing soil pH, soil nutrients, water retention, germination, or plant growth.

independent_variables.name: Lime amount added to soil
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing how liming affects soil pH or plant growth. The lime amount should be scaled to the mass of soil used.

independent_variables.name: Green manure percentage
independent_variables.notes: Possible for plant growth or soil nutrient investigations, but students need enough time for decomposition effects.

independent_variables.name: Hydroponic nutrient concentration
independent_variables.notes: Feasible with simple containers, seedlings, nutrient solution, and controlled light. Good for root length, shoot height, leaf number, or biomass.

independent_variables.name: Paint color
independent_variables.notes: Good for modelling urban heat island effects using surface temperature under sunlight or a lamp.

independent_variables.name: Support for biophilic design feature
independent_variables.notes: Use as a public support or perception survey, not as a vague claim that biophilic design improves wellbeing.

independent_variables.name: Awareness of air pollution problem
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing whether awareness is linked to support for green walls, pedestrianization, or air pollution strategies.

independent_variables.name: Previous gardening or farming experience
independent_variables.notes: Good for testing whether experience affects support for urban farming or school gardening projects.

independent_variables.name: Access to urban green space
independent_variables.notes: Can be paired with green space use frequency, perceived benefits, or support for green space expansion.

independent_variables.name: Total population
independent_variables.notes: Useful for comparing population size with total emissions, total waste, freshwater withdrawal, or energy demand. Per capita variables are often cleaner.

independent_variables.name: Population growth rate
independent_variables.notes: Better than birth rate or death rate alone for ESS topics about resource demand, urbanization, water stress, or food security.

independent_variables.name: Fertility rate
independent_variables.notes: Possible IV for development and resource-use investigations, but students must clearly link it to an environmental issue.

independent_variables.name: Birth rate
independent_variables.notes: Possible but weaker than population growth rate or fertility rate. Best used only with a clear resource-use or sustainability link.

independent_variables.name: Life expectancy
independent_variables.notes: Can be used as a development indicator when comparing environmental quality, access to clean energy, pollution, or water stress.

independent_variables.name: Child mortality rate
independent_variables.notes: Possible IV or context variable for water access, air pollution, sanitation, or development-related sustainability investigations.

independent_variables.name: Energy use per person
independent_variables.notes: Strong IV for carbon emissions, ecological footprint, development, and energy sustainability investigations.

independent_variables.name: Electricity from fossil fuels percentage
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing energy structure with CO₂ emissions, air pollution, or renewable energy transitions.

independent_variables.name: Electricity from renewables percentage
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing renewable electricity use with CO₂ emissions, energy access, or development indicators.

independent_variables.name: Electricity from bioenergy percentage
independent_variables.notes: Possible IV for energy-transition topics, but students should consider land-use and biomass sustainability issues.

independent_variables.name: Alternative and nuclear energy percentage
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing low-carbon energy share with CO₂ emissions or fossil fuel dependence.

independent_variables.name: Fossil fuel consumption subsidies
independent_variables.notes: Good for linking energy policy to fossil fuel use, emissions, or renewable energy transition.

independent_variables.name: Gini coefficient
independent_variables.notes: Possible IV for environmental justice topics, such as access to clean energy, water stress, pollution exposure, or ecological footprint.

independent_variables.name: Average years of schooling
independent_variables.notes: Possible IV for sustainability indicators, but students must avoid turning the IA into a purely education/development study.

independent_variables.name: Expected years of schooling
independent_variables.notes: Possible development IV for environmental behavior, energy access, or sustainability indicators.

independent_variables.name: Literacy rate
independent_variables.notes: Possible but often weaker than education index or years of schooling. Needs a clear ESS link.

independent_variables.name: Nitrogen fertilizer use
independent_variables.notes: Strong IV for eutrophication, nitrous oxide emissions, water pollution, or agricultural sustainability topics.

independent_variables.name: Phosphate fertilizer use
independent_variables.notes: Strong IV for eutrophication, water pollution, and agricultural runoff topics.

independent_variables.name: Meat consumption by type
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing diet patterns with agricultural emissions, land use, or ecological footprint.

independent_variables.name: Aquaculture production
independent_variables.notes: Good for comparing food production systems, fisheries pressure, or sustainable seafood transitions.

independent_variables.name: Capture fishery production
independent_variables.notes: Good for overfishing and marine resource-use investigations.

independent_variables.name: Sustainable fisheries GDP percentage
independent_variables.notes: Possible IV for marine sustainability or blue economy topics, but students need to understand what the indicator measures.

independent_variables.name: Small-scale fisheries access rights protection
independent_variables.notes: Useful for environmental justice and fisheries management topics, but the indicator may be harder for students to explain.

independent_variables.name: Agricultural land with acceptable soil degradation level
independent_variables.notes: Good for soil degradation, sustainable agriculture, and food security investigations if the dataset is clear.

dependent_variables.name: Dissolved oxygen
equipment: dissolved oxygen probe or test kit

dependent_variables.name: Canopy cover
equipment: canopy app or visual estimate grid

dependent_variables.name: Soil pH
equipment: soil pH meter or pH test kit

dependent_variables.name: Soil organic matter content
equipment: drying oven or simple proxy test

dependent_variables.name: Food miles
equipment: survey form or online product data

dependent_variables.name: Support for school food waste reduction program
Perceived access to green space score
Aquatic macroinvertebrate diversity
dependent_variables.unit: Simpson's Diversity Index or species richness

dependent_variables.name: Plant percentage frequency
dependent_variables.unit: % of quadrats where species is present

dependent_variables.name: Leaf litter invertebrate diversity
dependent_variables.unit: Simpson's Diversity Index or species richness

dependent_variables.name: Tree and shrub invertebrate diversity
dependent_variables.unit: Simpson's Diversity Index or species richness

dependent_variables.name: Biochemical oxygen demand
equipment: dissolved oxygen probe or test kit

dependent_variables.name: Primary productivity
dependent_variables.unit: mg O₂/L/day, biomass change, or productivity estimate
equipment: dissolved oxygen probe or biomass method

dependent_variables.name: Support for green walls or green roofs
Perceived wellbeing benefit of green space
Willingness to pay for urban greening
Per capita CO₂ emissions from coal
Per capita CO₂ emissions from gas
Per capita CO₂ emissions from transport
Per capita CO₂ emissions from domestic passenger flights
CO₂ emissions by fuel or industry
Death rate from indoor air pollution
Share of population living in urban areas
Level of water stress
dependent_variables.unit: % freshwater withdrawal as proportion of available freshwater resources
